title: Agents, Workflows, and the Control Loop · Mastery Check
summary: Compare a fixed workflow with an adaptive tool-using agent. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for an agent-system boundary map. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish predefined workflows from model-directed agents and choose the simpler sufficient architecture.
Model the observe, decide, act, and verify cycle with explicit state transitions.
Separate model context, application state, external state, and durable records.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""Bounded agent-loop portfolio. No external effects occur in this browser lab."""

steps.title: Diagnose the hidden failure
steps.instruction:
Use a changed quiz case derived from this module: loops foundations. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed quiz case derived from this module: loops foundations. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for state invariants that must hold before and after every loop iteration.

content.text: State invariants that must hold before and after every loop iteration. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Compare plausible approaches against the decision, constraints, evidence quality, and cost of being wrong.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: loops foundations. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Loop Invariants, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to an agent-system boundary map.

content.text: Only the final answer needs validation. This fails the Loop Invariants contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Loop Invariants applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: State invariants that must hold before and after every loop iteration.
